brief: GAP/RATIONALE: HeartMate 3 (HM3) = fully magnetically levitated LVAD; lowers thromboembolic events vs HeartMate II but bleeding events remain significant. Standard antithrombotic regimen after HM3 implant = warfarin + aspirin (ASA), but optimal antithrombotic therapy is unestablished/lacks evidence. | OBJECTIVE: Assess impact of combined ASA + warfarin vs warfarin alone on incidence of non-surgical bleeding in HM3 LVAD patients. | DESIGN: Systematic review + meta-analysis. Databases searched: MEDLINE, Embase, Cochrane. Included RCTs and observational studies comparing warfarin alone vs warfarin+ASA in HM3 LVAD patients. Binary endpoints analyzed as risk ratios (RR) with 95% CI; random-effects model for all endpoints. | INCLUDED STUDIES/SAMPLE: 5 studies total (1 RCT + 4 observational); 869 patients. 424 (48.8%) on warfarin alone. 662 (76.2%) male. | COMPARISON: warfarin alone vs warfarin + ASA combined regimen. | RESULTS (vs combined regimen, warfarin alone): non-surgical bleeding significantly reduced RR 0.30 (95% CI 0.09-0.95; p=0.04); GI bleeding significantly reduced RR 0.26 (95% CI 0.12-0.58; p<0.001); all-cause mortality no significant difference RR 1.02 (95% CI 0.45-2.32; p=0.963). | TAKEAWAY: Warfarin alone for anticoagulation in HM3 patients is associated with reduced bleeding risk vs combined warfarin+ASA therapy, with no mortality difference.
